reason: The text demonstrates God's deep concern for the Hebrew people (עַמִּי - 'my people') through multiple explicit statements: He has 'surely seen the affliction' (רָאֹה רָאִיתִי) of His people in Egypt, 'heard their cry' (צַעֲקָתָ֤ם שָׁמַ֙עְתִּי), and 'knows their sorrows' (יָדַ֖עְתִּי אֶת־ מַכְאֹבָֽיו). Most significantly, God declares 'I am come down to deliver them' (וָאֵרֵ֞ד לְהַצִּיל֣וֹ), actively intervening in their oppression. He further promises to bring them to a good, spacious land flowing with milk and honey, and commissions Moses specifically to lead this liberation. The repetition of personal knowledge and active compassion toward their suffering clearly indicates their importance to Him.
